symptoms.symptom_name: Newly-detected heart murmur at school screening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A murmur is the reason for presentation but is non-specific — it can be innocent or pathological until characterised in person.
The murmur mandates in-person characterisation to exclude structural disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Completely asymptomatic with normal exertion (competitive soccer without breathlessness, chest pain or syncope)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved, symptom-free exercise tolerance is strongly against a haemodynamically significant lesion.
Significant outflow obstruction typically produces exertional symptoms; their absence lowers its likelihood.

symptoms.symptom_name: No infancy cyanosis, poor feeding, failure to thrive or frequent chest infections

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a cyanotic/high-flow infancy history argues against significant congenital heart disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal growth and development in a thriving active child

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal growth and thriving are inconsistent with a chronically compromising cardiac lesion.

symptoms.symptom_name: No family history of inherited cardiac disease or sudden cardiac death

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative family history modestly lowers the pre-test probability of inherited cardiac disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, short grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur at the left lower sternal border that decreases on standing (Still's murmur)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The soft, short, low-pitched systolic murmur that softens on standing is the classic Still's-murmur signature — but it is a clinician auscultation finding sealed from the AI Doctor, obtainable only in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal physiological S2 splitting, no click/thrill/gallop, normal synchronous brachial and femoral pulses

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal (not fixed/wide) S2 splitting argues strongly against an ASD — a sealed clinician auscultation finding.
Normal-volume synchronous pulses and absence of a click/thrill argue against significant aortic stenosis or coarctation.

investigation_details.expected_result: Structurally normal heart, confirming an innocent murmur
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An echocardiogram is not routinely required to confirm a classic innocent murmur on in-person examination; it is reserved for atypical features or diagnostic uncertainty, or for definitive parental reassurance.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A structurally normal echocardiogram definitively excludes structural disease, but is a hospital/clinic investigation never available in real time via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical cardiovascular examination (auscultation, pulses, blood pressure)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most important next step — characterising the murmur in person is what distinguishes an innocent from a pathological murmur; appropriate on a routine (not emergency) timeframe given the child is asymptomatic with no red flags.
Not routinely indicated for a classic innocent murmur; reserved for atypical features, red flags, or definitive reassurance at the discretion of the reviewing clinician.
